> *Your visible true spending averaged **$7,744 per month**, with April elevated by a $3,850 property-tax payment.*

I counted actual purchases and bills from both checking and credit cards, while excluding transfers between your accounts and credit-card payments to avoid double-counting.

| Complete month | True spending |
|---|---:|
| April 2026 | $9,565.24 |
| May 2026 | $6,245.65 |
| June 2026 | $7,422.43 |
| **3-month average** | **$7,744.44** |

Your underlying monthly baseline was about **$6,461** after removing April’s $3,850 property-tax payment. However, property tax is still a real household cost, so **$7,744/month** is the better planning figure unless that payment was genuinely one-off.

This result covers the **three complete months available in the returned history—April through June 2026**. A longer window would provide a more stable estimate and spread annual or irregular bills across the correct number of months.
